description:
You are helping a (fictional) MLOps engineer who stores every experiment in its own folder under

/home/user/experiments/

Every experiment folder already contains a small INI-style configuration file called config.ini.

Your assignment:

1. Recursively scan /home/user/experiments/ for every file named “config.ini”.  
2. From each config.ini read the following five keys (all guaranteed to exist):  
   • metadata.experiment_name  
   • training.learning_rate  
   • training.batch_size  
   • results.accuracy  
   • metadata.timestamp  

3. Create a new directory, if it does not already exist:  
   /home/user/experiment_reports/

4. Inside that directory create a text file named  
   /home/user/experiment_reports/experiments_summary.log

   The file MUST be plain ASCII, UTF-8 encoded, and have exactly the following pipe-separated format (including the header line):

   experiment_name|learning_rate|batch_size|accuracy|run_timestamp  
   <row_1>  
   <row_2>  
   ...  

   Additional formatting requirements:  
   • One line per experiment, newline-terminated.  
   • The header line appears first.  
   • Sort the data rows alphabetically by experiment_name (ascending).  
   • learning_rate must keep any leading zeros and decimal points exactly as in the INI.  
   • batch_size is written as an integer.  
   • accuracy has exactly two decimal places.  
   • run_timestamp is copied verbatim from metadata.timestamp.  

5. Determine which experiment achieved the highest accuracy (if there is a tie, pick the alphabetically first experiment_name).  
   • Write a one-line file named /home/user/experiment_reports/best_accuracy.log whose exact content is:  

     experiment_name:<name> accuracy:<value>  

     …with no extra spaces or trailing newline.

6. Create (or update) a symbolic link called  
   /home/user/best_experiment  
   that points to the directory of the “best accuracy” experiment you selected in step 5.  The link must be relative so that if the whole /home/user tree is moved elsewhere, the symlink remains valid.

7. Everything must run without requiring sudo and must function even if the target directories already exist.

The automated grader will verify:
• The presence and exact byte-for-byte content of /home/user/experiment_reports/experiments_summary.log  
• The presence and content of /home/user/experiment_reports/best_accuracy.log  
• That /home/user/best_experiment is a valid relative symlink pointing to the correct experiment folder.

No other files should be modified. Use any standard command-line tools or short scripts of your choice, but do not hard-code the experiment names—the solution must work by actually parsing the INI files.
truth:
Initial directory tree before the agent starts:

/home/user/experiments/
└── exp_alpha
    └── config.ini
└── exp_beta
    └── config.ini
└── exp_gamma
    └── config.ini

Contents of each INI file:

/home/user/experiments/exp_alpha/config.ini
[metadata]
experiment_name=exp_alpha
timestamp=2023-08-23T15:34:12Z

[training]
learning_rate=0.001
batch_size=32

[results]
accuracy=0.88


/home/user/experiments/exp_beta/config.ini
[metadata]
experiment_name=exp_beta
timestamp=2023-08-24T09:18:45Z

[training]
learning_rate=0.0005
batch_size=64

[results]
accuracy=0.93


/home/user/experiments/exp_gamma/config.ini
[metadata]
experiment_name=exp_gamma
timestamp=2023-08-24T17:02:01Z

[training]
learning_rate=0.002
batch_size=32

[results]
accuracy=0.89


Expected artefacts after correct completion:

1. /home/user/experiment_reports/experiments_summary.log   (single newline at the end)
experiment_name|learning_rate|batch_size|accuracy|run_timestamp
exp_alpha|0.001|32|0.88|2023-08-23T15:34:12Z
exp_beta|0.0005|64|0.93|2023-08-24T09:18:45Z
exp_gamma|0.002|32|0.89|2023-08-24T17:02:01Z

2. /home/user/experiment_reports/best_accuracy.log   (no trailing newline)
experiment_name:exp_beta accuracy:0.93

3. /home/user/best_experiment  (symlink)
Relative path target: ../experiments/exp_beta
name: task_000000_7212f9c7
